# numopt4j

> High-performance Java numerical optimization library providing L-BFGS-B, SLSQP, and TRF algorithms.

numopt4j is a Java numerical optimization library supporting unconstrained optimization, bound-constrained optimization, equality/inequality constrained optimization, and nonlinear least-squares fitting.

## Algorithm Selection

- Unconstrained or bound-constrained optimization → L-BFGS-B (`LBFGSBProblem`)
- Equality or inequality constrained optimization → SLSQP (`SLSQPProblem`)
- Nonlinear least-squares fitting → TRF (`TRFProblem`)
- Scalar root-finding → Brentq (`FindRoot.scalar`)
- Multivariate root-finding → HYBR / Broyden (`FindRoot.equations`)
- Not sure which to use → `Minimize` (auto-selects)

## Quick Start

### Unconstrained optimization (auto-selects L-BFGS-B)

```java
OptimizationResult r = Minimize.objective(x -> x[0]*x[0] + x[1]*x[1])
    .startingFrom(1.0, 1.0)
    .run();

if (r.isSuccessful()) {
    System.out.println(Arrays.toString(r.getSolution())); // [0.0, 0.0]
} else {
    System.out.println(r.getErrorMessage());
}
```

### Bound-constrained optimization (L-BFGS-B)

```java
OptimizationResult r = LBFGSBProblem.create()
    .objective(x -> x[0]*x[0] + x[1]*x[1])
    .bounds(Bound.atLeast(0.5), Bound.atLeast(0.5))
    .initialPoint(1.0, 1.0)
    .solve();
```

### Equality-constrained optimization (SLSQP)

```java
// minimize x[0] + x[1], subject to x[0]^2 + x[1]^2 = 1
OptimizationResult r = SLSQPProblem.create()
    .objective(x -> x[0] + x[1])
    .equalityConstraints(x -> x[0]*x[0] + x[1]*x[1] - 1)
    .initialPoint(0.5, 0.5)
    .solve();
```

### Nonlinear least squares (TRF)

```java
OptimizationResult r = Minimize.leastSquares((x, res) -> {
        res[0] = x[0] - 3.0;
        res[1] = x[1] - 4.0;
    }, 2)
    .startingFrom(0.0, 0.0)
    .run();
```

### Scalar root-finding (Brentq)

```java
// Find root of sin(x) in [3, 4] → π
OptimizationResult r = FindRoot.scalar(Math::sin)
    .bracket(3.0, 4.0)
    .solve();
double root = r.getRoot(); // ≈ π
```

### Multivariate root-finding (HYBR / Broyden)

```java
// Default: HYBR
OptimizationResult r = FindRoot.equations((x, f) -> {
        f[0] = x[0]*x[0] - 2;
        f[1] = x[1] - x[0];
    }, 2)
    .initialPoint(1.0, 1.0)
    .solve();
double[] solution = r.getSolution(); // [√2, √2]

// Use Broyden explicitly
FindRoot.equations(fn, 2).initialPoint(1.0, 1.0).method(RootMethod.BROYDEN).solve();

// Use central-difference Jacobian (HYBR only)
FindRoot.equations(fn, 2).jacobian(NumericalJacobian.CENTRAL).initialPoint(1.0, 1.0).solve();
```

## Core API

### Minimize (unified entry point, auto-selects algorithm)

```java
Minimize.objective(ToDoubleFunction<double[]> f)   // recommended: no gradient needed
Minimize.objective(Univariate f)                   // analytic gradient
Minimize.leastSquares(BiConsumer<double[],double[]> residuals, int m)
Minimize.leastSquares(Multivariate f, int m)
```

### LBFGSBProblem (L-BFGS-B bound-constrained quasi-Newton)

```java
LBFGSBProblem.create()
    .objective(ToDoubleFunction<double[]>)  // auto-uses NumericalGradient.CENTRAL
    .objective(Univariate)                  // analytic gradient
    .initialPoint(double...)
    .bounds(Bound...)
    .maxIterations(int)                     // default 100
    .maxEvaluations(int)                    // default 1000
    .gradientTolerance(double)              // default √ε ≈ 1.5e-8
    .functionTolerance(double)              // default 1e7 (factr)
    .corrections(int)                       // L-BFGS correction count, default 7
    .solve()                                // → OptimizationResult
```

### SLSQPProblem (SLSQP sequential least-squares programming)

```java
SLSQPProblem.create()
    .objective(ToDoubleFunction<double[]> / Univariate)
    .equalityConstraints(ToDoubleFunction<double[]>...)   // c(x) = 0
    .inequalityConstraints(ToDoubleFunction<double[]>...) // c(x) >= 0
    .initialPoint(double...)
    .bounds(Bound...)
    .accuracy(double)                       // default 1e-6
    .maxIterations(int)                     // default 100
    .maxEvaluations(int)                    // default 1000
    .solve()                                // → OptimizationResult
```

### TRFProblem (TRF trust-region reflective least squares)

```java
TRFProblem.create()
    .residuals(BiConsumer<double[],double[]>, int m)  // auto-uses NumericalJacobian.CENTRAL
    .objective(Multivariate)                          // analytic Jacobian
    .initialPoint(double...)
    .bounds(Bound...)
    .functionTolerance(double)              // default 1e-8
    .coefficientTolerance(double)           // default 1e-8
    .gradientTolerance(double)              // default 1e-8
    .maxEvaluations(int)                    // default 100*n
    .solve()                                // → OptimizationResult
```

### FindRoot (unified root-finding entry point)

```java
FindRoot.scalar(DoubleUnaryOperator f)                          // 1-D, returns RootFinder
FindRoot.equations(BiConsumer<double[],double[]> fn, int n)     // N-D, returns RootFinder
```

### RootFinder (fluent builder)

```java
RootFinder.create()
    // 1-D
    .function(DoubleUnaryOperator)      // set scalar function
    .bracket(double a, double b)        // set interval (required for Brentq)
    // N-D
    .equations(BiConsumer, int n)       // set system F(x) = 0
    .jacobian(NumericalJacobian)        // optional, specify difference type (HYBR only)
    .initialPoint(double...)            // set initial point (required)
    // common
    .method(RootMethod)                 // optional: BRENTQ / HYBR / BROYDEN
    .tolerance(xtol, rtol)              // Brentq convergence tolerances
    .functionTolerance(ftol)            // N-D convergence tolerance
    .maxIterations(int)
    .solve()                            // → OptimizationResult
    .alloc()                            // → RootWorkspace (for reuse)
```

### OptimizationResult

```java
result.isSuccessful()       // whether converged
result.getRoot()            // 1-D root value (NaN for N-D)
result.getSolution()        // solution vector (double[], never null)
result.getObjectiveValue()  // objective function value
result.getIterations()      // iteration count
result.getEvaluations()     // function evaluation count
result.getSummary()         // single-line summary string
result.getErrorMessage()    // reason + suggestion on failure, null otherwise
result.toString()           // multi-line structured diagnostic output
```

## Documentation

- [llms-full.txt](llms-full.txt): Full API reference (for deep AI understanding)
- META-INF/AI_GUIDE.md: Built-in AI usage guide in JAR (accessible via Context7)
- [GitHub Wiki](https://github.com/curioloop/numopt4j/wiki/): Detailed error code fix guides
